message:
test(tcp_tls): add TCP/TLS benchmark and concurrency test suite for asupersync (wa-1u55z)

Introduces two new test/benchmark files exercising the asupersync TCP
and TLS networking primitives under the distributed feature, plus the
rcgen dev-dependency for runtime TLS certificate generation in tests.

New files:
- benches/tcp_tls.rs (321 lines): Criterion microbenchmarks measuring
  TCP connect latency, TLS handshake latency (both standard and mTLS),
  and throughput for 4MB bulk transfers over TLS. Uses loopback sockets
  with runtime-generated certificates via rcgen. Parameterized across
  payload sizes for throughput scaling analysis.
- tests/tcp_tls_labruntime.rs (206 lines): Integration tests for
  multi-client concurrent TLS handshakes and graceful shutdown races.
  Verifies that the asupersync TLS acceptor correctly handles N
  simultaneous connections and that shutdown ordering (client-first vs
  server-first) doesn't leak resources or deadlock. Uses rcgen CA
  certificates with proper key usage extensions for realistic mTLS.

Also:
- bench_common.rs: adds tcp_tls/ CI budget entry (500ms ceiling) to
  catch regressions in networking benchmarks on CI runners
- Cargo.lock: adds rcgen crate for test certificate generation

Both files are gated behind #[cfg(all(feature = "asupersync-runtime",
feature = "distributed"))] so they only compile/run when the full
async migration stack is active.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
